$comment: Normative appendix for L1-CA-07; extracted from protocols/pact-completion-authority.md (rejection record shape) at plugin 4.6.8 / commit b4041ccf. One schema serves both rejection kinds — gate (teachback) and work (HANDOFF). Normatively stronger than the as-built realization, which enforced no shape validator at the fork point; conformance annexes record this as satisfied-with-deviation.
title: Rejection record
description: A Lead's durable rejection of a teachback or a HANDOFF. Shape-only: the re-submission lifecycle lives in the prose requirements L1-TB-04 and L1-CA-07.

corrections.description: The specific corrections the revised submission is expected to make.

since.description: Timezone-aware ISO-8601 timestamp of the rejection. Naive (zone-less) timestamps are invalid; the trailing-offset pattern enforces this mechanically.

revision_number.description: Rejection-cycle counter. The Lead writes 1 at first rejection; each re-submission increments it. Feeds the escalation threshold of L1-TB-05.

examples.reason: Teachback omits the idempotency constraint that motivated the dispatch
corrections: Restate the retry model and why the worker must be idempotent
Name the assumption about queue delivery semantics being made
